perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, this Agreement is created for the purpose of preventing the unauthorized disclosure of the confidential and proprietary information . The Parties agree as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, this Agreement is created for the purpose of preventing the unauthorized disclosure of the confidential and proprietary information . The legal entities agree as follows:
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Parties" with "legal entities" creates ambiguity. Are "Parties" and "legal entities" synonymous? It contradicts the definition in Section I where 'Parties' is clearly defined as '1st Party' and '2nd Party'. This means obligations that originally applied to "1st Party" and "2nd Party" now might be enforced to legal entities, confusing the subject of the contract
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence at all times and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to kno w” basis. If any such Confidential Information shall reach a third (3rd) party, or become public, all liability will be on the Party that is responsible. Neither Party shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confid ential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within ____ days. This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Unilateral as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Parties,' which, according to the explanation and the contradicted text, has a specific meaning ('1st Party' and '2nd Party'). The changed text replaces 'Parties' with 'legal entities.' This creates ambiguity because it's unclear if 'legal entities' is synonymous with 'Parties'. The contradicted text defines the obligations of 'Parties', and by changing the term to 'legal entities', it becomes uncertain whether those same obligations apply to 'legal entities'. Because the definition of 'Parties' in Section I is not the same as 'legal entities' it creates a strong contradiction with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Party A’s relationship to Party B can be described as _________________________ and Party B’s relationship to Party A can be described as _________________________.
perturbation.changed_text: The Senior Partner's relationship to the Junior Partner can be described as _________________________ and the Junior Partner’s relationship to the Senior Partner can be described as _________________________.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Party A" and "Party B" with "Senior Partner" and "Junior Partner" introduces new terms not previously defined. If "Party A" and "Party B" are supposed to be equivalent to "1st Party" and "2nd Party," as suggested by the structure, this creates an in-text contradiction. This introduces confusion on what these relationships are and to whom do they apply. Is it between the senior and junior partner or the first and second parties
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Party A" and "Party B" with "Senior Partner" and "Junior Partner." The explanation states this introduces new terms not previously defined. The contradicted text refers to 'The Parties,' and if 'Party A' and 'Party B' were meant to align with '1st Party' and '2nd Party', then replacing them with 'Senior Partner' and 'Junior Partner' introduces a contradiction regarding which parties the agreement applies to. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology, which leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The 'parties' defined in the contradicted text now could be misinterpreted.

perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, the ter m “Confidential Information” shall include, but not be limited to, documents, records, information and data (whether verbal, electronic or written), drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, schedules, product plans, marketing plans, technical proced ures, manufacturing processes, analyses, compilations, studies, software, prototypes, samples, formulas, methodologies, formulations, product developments, patent applications, know -how, experimental results, specifications and other business information, relating to the Party’s business, assets, operations or contracts, furnished to the other Party and/or the other Party’s affiliates, employees, officers, owners, agents, consultants or representatives, in the course of their work contemplated in this Agree ment, regardless of whether such Confidential Information has been expressly designated as confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, the ter m “Proprietary Data” shall include, but not be limited to, documents, records, information and data (whether verbal, electronic or written), drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, schedules, product plans, marketing plans, technical proced ures, manufacturing processes, analyses, compilations, studies, software, prototypes, samples, formulas, methodologies, formulations, product developments, patent applications, know -how, experimental results, specifications and other business information, relating to the Party’s business, assets, operations or contracts, furnished to the other Party and/or the other Party’s affiliates, employees, officers, owners, agents, consultants or representatives, in the course of their work contemplated in this Agree ment, regardless of whether such Confidential Information has been expressly designated as confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Information” to “Proprietary Data” while still referencing "Confidential Information" in the last line of the definition creates confusion. This contradicts the intention of Section IV, which is to define what the Confidential Information is. Now it is only defining the Proprietary Data and referencing confidential information that is not defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence at all times and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to kno w” basis. If any such Confidential Information shall reach a third (3rd) party, or become public, all liability will be on the Party that is responsible. Neither Party shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confid ential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within ____ days. This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Unilateral as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data' in the definition, but the last line of the definition still references 'Confidential Information.' This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which outlines obligations specifically related to 'Confidential Information.' The change introduces ambiguity and inconsistency, as the contradicted text relies on a clear definition of 'Confidential Information,' which is no longer present in the changed text, leading to uncertainty about which data the obligations apply to. The definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' states that this type of contradiction occurs when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. This change aligns with this definition.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence at all times and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to kno w” basis.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Secret Information in the strictest of confidence at all times and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to observe” basis.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Information” to “Secret Information” and “need to know” to “need to observe” introduces new terms without definitions. This contradicts the definition of what Confidential Information is, as stated in Section IV. This creates confusion regarding what information the parties are obligated to protect, and to whom they can disclose it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, the ter m “Confidential Information” shall include, but not be limited to, documents, records, information and data (whether verbal, electronic or written), drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, schedules, product plans, marketing plans, technical proced ures, manufacturing processes, analyses, compilations, studies, software, prototypes, samples, formulas, methodologies, formulations, product developments, patent applications, know -how, experimental results, specifications and other business information, relating to the Party’s business, assets, operations or contracts, furnished to the other Party and/or the other Party’s affiliates, employees, officers, owners, agents, consultants or representatives, in the course of their work contemplated in this Agree ment, regardless of whether such Confidential Information has been expressly designated as confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Confidential Information" with "Secret Information" and "need to know" with "need to observe". The contradicted text defines "Confidential Information". Because the changed text uses new, undefined terms ('Secret Information' and 'need to observe'), it creates a contradiction because it deviates from and fails to align with the established definition of 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted text, leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about what information is protected. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The bounded Party’s(ies’) duty to hold the Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until such information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or written notice is given releasing such Party from this Agreem ent.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligated Person's duty to hold the Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until such information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or verbal notice is given releasing such Person from this Agreem ent.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "bounded Party’s(ies’)" with "obligated Person's" and "written notice" with "verbal notice" creates inconsistency. The original text has "Party" defined in Section I, but "Person" is nowhere. This introduces ambiguity and in-text contradiction. The term Person is not defined and the enforcement of the written notice is changed to verbal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings. This Agreement may not be amended except in w riting with the acknowledgment of the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'bounded Party’s(ies’)' with 'obligated Person's' and 'written notice' with 'verbal notice'. The original text defines 'Party' in Section I, but 'Person' is undefined. The contradicted text states that the agreement can only be amended in writing. The change to 'verbal notice' directly contradicts this, as a verbal notice is not a written amendment, thus causing an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition provided, as the contract uses terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
